text: At 2026-05-16T20:42:11, IIS on WEBAPP01.corp.local received a POST request to "/upload.aspx" (query: file=shell.aspx) from IP 198.51.100.77, returning HTTP 200. Uploading files via web forms to server-accessible paths is the primary webshell deployment vector (T1505.003 (Server Software Component: Web Shell)). At 2026-05-16T20:43:02, user "IIS APPPOOL\DefaultAppPool" on WEBAPP01.corp.local launched PowerShell with suspicious flags: -NoProfile, -WindowStyle Hidden, -EncodedCommand (base64 obfuscated payload). The parent process was the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe), confirming this PowerShell was spawned by the previously uploaded webshell. The use of encoded commands and hidden window mode is a strong indicator of obfuscated malware execution or post-exploitation activity (T1059.001 (Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell)). At 2026-05-16T20:43:08, a suspicious executable "C:\Temp\payload.exe" was launched by user "NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM" with command line "C:\Temp\payload.exe --silent". Executables dropped in temporary directories and run silently are characteristic of malware deployment (T1105 (Ingress Tool Transfer)). At 2026-05-16T20:43:05, process "powershell.exe" initiated a TCP connection to 198.51.100.77:8080. Outbound connections from PowerShell to external IPs are a hallmark of C2 communication (T1071.001 (Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols)). At 2026-05-16T20:42:14, file "C:\inetpub\wwwroot\uploads\shell.aspx" was created by "w3wp.exe" (user: IIS APPPOOL\DefaultAppPool). The sequence of events on WEBAPP01.corp.local represents a complete attack chain: initial access, code execution, C2 establishment, and persistence. Immediate incident response is recommended.
